     The Numbers Business Report

Flatliners (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $16,883,115Details
International Box Office $28,290,623
Worldwide Box Office $45,173,738
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,269,871
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $839,376
Total $2,109,247
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Five medical students, hoping to gain insight into the mystery of what lies beyond the confines of life, embark on a daring and dangerous experiment. By stopping their hearts for short periods of time, each triggers a near-death experience. As the investigation becomes more and more perilous, they are forced to confront the sins of their pasts, as well as contend with the paranormal consequences of trespassing to the other side.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,574,326 (38.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.57 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,552 opening theaters/2,552 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $22,510,825

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 29th, 2017 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: September 27th, 2017 (Wide) (Trinidad)
September 27th, 2017 (Wide) (Jamaica)
September 28th, 2017 (Wide) (Oman)
September 28th, 2017 (Wide) (Bahrain)
September 28th, 2017 (Wide) (Aruba)
Video Release: December 12th, 2017 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence and terror, sexual content, language, thematic material, and some drug references.
(Rating bulletin 2493 (Cert #50627), 9/13/2017)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Franchise: Flatliners
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel, Reboot
Plot point: Death of Other Extended Family, Human Experimentation, Medical Students, Near Death Experience
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Psychological Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Further Films, Laurence Mark, Cross Creek Pictures, The Safran Company
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
